description: Advanced GLSL shader techniques in TouchDesigner including raymarching, signed distance fields, reaction-diffusion simulations, feedback loops, and procedural texture generation.

techniques.name: Basic Raymarching with SDF
techniques.description: Raymarching through a scene defined by signed distance functions (SDFs). Renders 3D geometry entirely inside a GLSL TOP without any SOP geometry.
techniques.notes: Use GLSL TOP in Pixel shader mode. Set Cook Rate to 'Frame' for animation. Output is a full 3D rendered image.

operators_needed.purpose: Drive uTime uniform with absTime.seconds expression

techniques.name: SDF Domain Repetition and Deformation
techniques.description: Infinite repetition of SDF primitives using mod() and domain warping for complex procedural scenes.

techniques.name: Gray-Scott Reaction-Diffusion
techniques.description: Classic Gray-Scott reaction-diffusion system generating organic patterns like coral, spots, and labyrinths. Runs entirely on GPU via ping-pong Feedback TOP.
techniques.notes: Requires Feedback TOP for ping-pong buffer. Wire: GLSL TOP -> Feedback TOP -> back to GLSL TOP input. Run at cook rate 'Frame'. Parameter F and K control pattern type: spots (F=0.035,K=0.065), labyrinths (F=0.06,K=0.062), coral (F=0.055,K=0.062).

visualization_shader.description: Second GLSL TOP to colorize the A/B state into a visible image

operators_needed.purpose: Seed initial state — connect to GLSL TOP input 0 before Feedback is active
Ping-pong buffer — output feeds back to GLSL TOP input 0

techniques.name: Feedback Loop Effects
techniques.description: Multi-pass feedback effects using Feedback TOP to create trails, echo, zoom-blur, kaleidoscope feedback, and flame simulation.
techniques.notes: The Feedback TOP outputs its previous frame. Connect source -> Feedback TOP. Then use the Feedback TOP output as one input to your effect shader alongside the new source frame.

techniques.name: Procedural FBM Noise Texture
techniques.description: Fractional Brownian Motion (fBm) layered noise for generating clouds, terrain heightmaps, marble, and wood grain procedurally.

techniques.name: Voronoi / Cellular Noise
techniques.description: Worley/Voronoi noise for cellular patterns, cracked earth, skin, stone, and mosaic effects.

techniques.name: Multi-Pass GLSL with GLSL Multi TOP
techniques.description: Using GLSL Multi TOP to run multiple shader passes (G-buffer, lighting, post-process) in a single node with multiple outputs.
techniques.notes: GLSL Multi TOP outputs up to 8 textures per cook. Pixel shader can write to multiple outputs via layout(location=N). Inputs are accessed as sTD2DInputs[N].
